NKJV.1: And He entered the synagogue again, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
NKJV.2: So they watched Him closely, whether He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him.
NKJV.3: And He said to the man who had the withered hand, “Step forward.”
NKJV.4: Then He said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?” But they kept silent.
NKJV.5: And when He had looked around at them with anger, being grieved by the hardness of their hearts, He said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored as whole as the other.
NKJV.6: Then the Pharisees went out and immediately plotted with the Herodians against Him, how they might destroy Him.
NKJV.7: But Jesus withdrew with His disciples to the sea. And a great multitude from Galilee followed Him, and from Judea
NKJV.8: and Jerusalem and Idumea and beyond the Jordan; and those from Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they heard how many things He was doing, came to Him.
NKJV.9: So He told His disciples that a small boat should be kept ready for Him because of the multitude, lest they should crush Him.
NKJV.10: For He healed many, so that as many as had afflictions pressed about Him to touch Him.
NKJV.11: And the unclean spirits, whenever they saw Him, fell down before Him and cried out, saying, “You are the Son of God.”
NKJV.12: But He sternly warned them that they should not make Him known.
NKJV.13: And He went up on the mountain and called to Him those He Himself wanted. And they came to Him.
NKJV.14: Then He appointed twelve, that they might be with Him and that He might send them out to preach,
NKJV.15: and to have power to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:
NKJV.16: Simon, to whom He gave the name Peter;
NKJV.17: James the son of Zebedee and John the brother of James, to whom He gave the name Boanerges, that is, “Sons of Thunder”;
NKJV.18: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Cananite;
NKJV.19: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed Him. And they went into a house.
NKJV.20: Then the multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
NKJV.21: But when His own people heard about this, they went out to lay hold of Him, for they said, “He is out of His mind.”
NKJV.22: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebub,” and, “By the ruler of the demons He casts out demons.”
NKJV.23: So He called them to Himself and said to them in parables: “How can Satan cast out Satan?
NKJV.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
NKJV.25: And if a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
NKJV.26: And if Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he cannot stand, but has an end.
NKJV.27: No one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man. And then he will plunder his house.
NKJV.28: “Assuredly, I say to you, all sins will be forgiven the sons of men, and whatever blasphemies they may utter;
NKJV.29: but he who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation”—
NKJV.30: because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
NKJV.31: Then His brothers and His mother came, and standing outside they sent to Him, calling Him.
NKJV.32: And a multitude was sitting around Him; and they said to Him, “Look, Your mother and Your brothers are outside seeking You.”
NKJV.33: But He answered them, saying, “Who is My mother, or My brothers?”
NKJV.34: And He looked around in a circle at those who sat about Him, and said, “Here are My mother and My brothers!
NKJV.35: For whoever does the  will of God is My brother and My sister and mother.”

ESV.1: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there with a withered hand.
ESV.2: And they watched Jesus,  to see whether he would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
ESV.3: And he said to the man with the withered hand, “Come here.”
ESV.4: And he said to them,  “Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
ESV.5: And he looked around at them with anger, grieved at their hardness of heart, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
ESV.6: The Pharisees went out and immediately held counsel with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
ESV.7: Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the sea, and a great crowd followed, from Galilee and Judea
ESV.8: and Jerusalem and Idumea and from beyond the Jordan and from around Tyre and Sidon. When the great crowd heard all that he was doing, they came to him.
ESV.9: And he told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, lest they crush him,
ESV.10: for he had healed many, so that all who had diseases pressed around him to touch him.
ESV.11: And whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God.”
ESV.12: And he strictly ordered them not to make him known.
ESV.13: And he went up on the mountain and called to him those whom he desired, and they came to him.
ESV.14: And he appointed twelve (whom he also named apostles) so that they might be with him and he might send them out to preach
ESV.15: and have authority to cast out demons.
ESV.16: He appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
ESV.17: James the son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
ESV.18: Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Zealot,
ESV.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
ESV.20: Then he went home, and the crowd gathered again, so that they could not even eat.
ESV.21: And when his family heard it, they went out to seize him, for they were saying, “He is out of his mind.”
ESV.22: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and “by the prince of demons he casts out the demons.”
ESV.23: And he called them to him and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
ESV.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
ESV.25: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
ESV.26: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but is coming to an end.
ESV.27: But no one can enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man. Then indeed he may plunder his house.
ESV.28: “Truly, I say to you, all sins will be forgiven the children of man, and whatever blasphemies they utter,
ESV.29: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin” —
ESV.30: for they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.”
ESV.31: And his mother and his brothers came, and standing outside they sent to him and called him.
ESV.32: And a crowd was sitting around him, and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers  are outside, seeking you.”
ESV.33: And he answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
ESV.34: And looking about at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
ESV.35: For whoever does the will of God, he is my brother and sister and mother.”

NASB.1: He entered again into a synagogue; and a man was there whose hand was withered.
NASB.2: They were watching Him [to see] if He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him.
NASB.3: He said to the man with the withered hand, 'Get up and come forward!'
NASB.4: And He said to them, 'Is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the Sabbath, to save a life or to kill?' But they kept silent.
NASB.5: After looking around at them with anger, grieved at their hardness of heart, He said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
NASB.6: The Pharisees went out and immediately [began] conspiring with the Herodians against Him, [as] [to] how they might destroy Him.
NASB.7: Jesus withdrew to the sea with His disciples; and a great multitude from Galilee followed; and [also] from Judea,
NASB.8: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumea, and beyond the Jordan, and the vicinity of Tyre and Sidon, a great number of people heard of all that He was doing and came to Him.
NASB.9: And He told His disciples that a boat should stand ready for Him because of the crowd, so that they would not crowd Him;
NASB.10: for He had healed many, with the result that all those who had afflictions pressed around Him in order to touch Him.
NASB.11: Whenever the unclean spirits saw Him, they would fall down before Him and shout, 'You are the Son of God!'
NASB.12: And He earnestly warned them not to tell who He was.
NASB.13: And He went up on the mountain and summoned those whom He Himself wanted, and they came to Him.
NASB.14: And He appointed twelve, so that they would be with Him and that He [could] send them out to preach,
NASB.15: and to have authority to cast out the demons.
NASB.16: And He appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom He gave the name Peter),
NASB.17: and James, the [son] of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (to them He gave the name Boanerges, which means, 'Sons of Thunder ');
NASB.18: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Zealot;
NASB.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Him.
NASB.20: And He came home, and the crowd gathered again, to such an extent that they could not even eat a meal.
NASB.21: When His own people heard [of] [this], they went out to take custody of Him; for they were saying, 'He has lost His senses.'
NASB.22: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, 'He is possessed by Beelzebul,' and 'He casts out the demons by the ruler of the demons.'
NASB.23: And He called them to Himself and began speaking to them in parables, 'How can Satan cast out Satan?
NASB.24: 'If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
NASB.25: 'If a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
NASB.26: 'If Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but he is finished!
NASB.27: 'But no one can enter the strong man's house and plunder his property unless he first binds the strong man, and then he will plunder his house.
NASB.28: 'Truly I say to you, all sins shall be forgiven the sons of men, and whatever blasphemies they utter;
NASB.29: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin '--
NASB.30: because they were saying, 'He has an unclean spirit.'
NASB.31: Then His mother and His brothers arrived, and standing outside they sent [word] to Him and called Him.
NASB.32: A crowd was sitting around Him, and they said to Him, 'Behold, Your mother and Your brothers are outside looking for You.'
NASB.33: Answering them, He said, 'Who are My mother and My brothers?'
NASB.34: Looking about at those who were sitting around Him, He said, 'Behold My mother and My brothers!
NASB.35: 'For whoever does the will of God, he is My brother and sister and mother.'

NLT.1: Jesus went into the synagogue again and noticed a man with a deformed hand.
NLT.2: Since it was the Sabbath, Jesus’ enemies watched him closely. If he healed the man’s hand, they planned to accuse him of working on the Sabbath.
NLT.3: Jesus said to the man with the deformed hand, “Come and stand in front of everyone.”
NLT.4: Then he turned to his critics and asked, “Does the law permit good deeds on the Sabbath, or is it a day for doing evil? Is this a day to save life or to destroy it?” But they wouldn’t answer him.
NLT.5: He looked around at them angrily and was deeply saddened by their hard hearts. Then he said to the man, “Hold out your hand.” So the man held out his hand, and it was restored!
NLT.6: At once the Pharisees went away and met with the supporters of Herod to plot how to kill Jesus.
NLT.7: Jesus went out to the lake with his disciples, and a large crowd followed him. They came from all over Galilee, Judea,
NLT.8: Jerusalem, Idumea, from east of the Jordan River, and even from as far north as Tyre and Sidon. The news about his miracles had spread far and wide, and vast numbers of people came to see him.
NLT.9: Jesus instructed his disciples to have a boat ready so the crowd would not crush him.
NLT.10: He had healed many people that day, so all the sick people eagerly pushed forward to touch him.
NLT.11: And whenever those possessed by evil spirits caught sight of him, the spirits would throw them to the ground in front of him shrieking, “You are the Son of God!”
NLT.12: But Jesus sternly commanded the spirits not to reveal who he was.
NLT.13: Afterward Jesus went up on a mountain and called out the ones he wanted to go with him. And they came to him.
NLT.14: Then he appointed twelve of them and called them his apostles. They were to accompany him, and he would send them out to preach,
NLT.15: giving them authority to cast out demons.
NLT.16: These are the twelve he chose: Simon (whom he named Peter),
NLT.17: James and John (the sons of Zebedee, but Jesus nicknamed them “Sons of Thunder” ),
NLT.20: One time Jesus entered a house, and the crowds began to gather again. Soon he and his disciples couldn’t even find time to eat.
NLT.21: When his family heard what was happening, they tried to take him away. “He’s out of his mind,” they said.
NLT.22: But the teachers of religious law who had arrived from Jerusalem said, “He’s possessed by Satan, the prince of demons. That’s where he gets the power to cast out demons.”
NLT.23: Jesus called them over and responded with an illustration. “How can Satan cast out Satan?” he asked.
NLT.24: “A kingdom divided by civil war will collapse.
NLT.25: Similarly, a family splintered by feuding will fall apart.
NLT.26: And if Satan is divided and fights against himself, how can he stand? He would never survive.
NLT.27: Let me illustrate this further. Who is powerful enough to enter the house of a strong man and plunder his goods? Only someone even stronger — someone who could tie him up and then plunder his house.
NLT.28: “I tell you the truth, all sin and blasphemy can be forgiven,
NLT.29: but anyone who blasphemes the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. This is a sin with eternal consequences.”
NLT.30: He told them this because they were saying, “He’s possessed by an evil spirit.”
NLT.31: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers came to see him. They stood outside and sent word for him to come out and talk with them.
NLT.32: There was a crowd sitting around Jesus, and someone said, “Your mother and your brothers are outside asking for you.”
NLT.33: Jesus replied, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”
NLT.34: Then he looked at those around him and said, “Look, these are my mother and brothers.
NLT.35: Anyone who does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”

WEB.1: He entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man there who had his hand withered.
WEB.2: They watched him, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him.
WEB.3: He said to the man who had his hand withered, “Stand up.”
WEB.4: He said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?” But they were silent.
WEB.5: When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.”  He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other.
WEB.6: The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
WEB.7: Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples, and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea,
WEB.8: from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.
WEB.9: He spoke to his disciples that a little boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they wouldn’t press on him.
WEB.10: For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him.
WEB.11: The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, “You are the Son of God!”
WEB.12: He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.
WEB.13: He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him.
WEB.14: He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach,
WEB.15: and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:
WEB.16: Simon, to whom he gave the name Peter;
WEB.17: James the son of Zebedee; John, the brother of James, and he called them Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder;
WEB.18: Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot;
WEB.19: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him. He came into a house.
WEB.20: The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
WEB.21: When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him: for they said, “He is insane.”
WEB.22: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons.”
WEB.23: He summoned them, and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
WEB.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
WEB.25: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
WEB.26: If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end.
WEB.27: But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder, unless he first binds the strong man; and then he will plunder his house.
WEB.28: Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;
WEB.29: but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.”
WEB.30: — because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
WEB.31: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.
WEB.32: A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, “Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters  are outside looking for you.”
WEB.33: He answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
WEB.34: Looking around at those who sat around him, he said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!
WEB.35: For whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.”

CSB17.1: Jesus <sup>ⓖ</sup> entered the synagogue <sup>ⓗ</sup> again, and a man was there who had a shriveled <sup>ⓘ</sup> hand.
CSB17.2: In order to accuse <sup>ⓙ</sup> him, they were watching him closely <sup>ⓚ</sup> to see whether he would heal <sup>ⓛ</sup> him on the Sabbath. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: He told the man with the shriveled hand, “Stand before us.”
CSB17.4: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful <sup>ⓝ</sup> to do good <sup>ⓐ</sup> on the Sabbath or to do evil, <sup>ⓑ</sup> to save life <sup>ⓒ</sup> or to kill?” <sup>ⓓ</sup> But they were silent. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.5: After looking around at them with anger, <sup>ⓕ</sup> he was grieved at the hardness <sup>ⓖ</sup> of their hearts and told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and his hand was restored. <sup>ⓗ</sup>

CSB17.7: Jesus <sup>ⓜ</sup> departed with his disciples to the sea, and a large crowd followed from Galilee, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and a large crowd followed from Judea, <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.8: Jerusalem, <sup>ⓑ</sup> Idumea, beyond the Jordan, <sup>ⓒ</sup> and around Tyre <sup>ⓓ</sup> and Sidon. <sup>ⓔ</sup> The large crowd came to him because they heard about everything he was doing.
CSB17.9: Then he told his disciples to have a small boat <sup>ⓕ</sup> ready for him, so that the crowd wouldn’t crush him.
CSB17.10: Since he had healed <sup>ⓖ</sup> many, all who had diseases were pressing toward him to touch <sup>ⓗ</sup> him.
CSB17.11: Whenever the unclean <sup>ⓘ</sup> spirits <sup>ⓙ</sup> saw him, they fell down <sup>ⓚ</sup> before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God!” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.12: And he would strongly warn <sup>ⓜ</sup> them not to make him known. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.13: Jesus <sup>ⓐ</sup> went up the mountain <sup>ⓑ</sup> and summoned those he wanted, and they came to him.
CSB17.14: He appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> to be with him, to send them out to preach, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.15: and to have authority to <sup>[2]</sup> drive out demons.
CSB17.16: He appointed the Twelve: <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> To Simon, <sup>ⓕ</sup> he gave the name Peter; <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.17: and to James <sup>ⓗ</sup> the son of Zebedee, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and to his brother John, <sup>ⓙ</sup> he gave the name “Boanerges” (that is, “Sons of Thunder” <sup>ⓚ</sup>);
CSB17.18: Andrew; <sup>ⓛ</sup> Philip <sup>ⓜ</sup> and Bartholomew; Matthew <sup>ⓝ</sup> and Thomas; <sup>ⓐ</sup> James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot,
CSB17.19: and Judas Iscariot, <sup>ⓑ</sup> who also betrayed him.
CSB17.20: Jesus entered a house, and the crowd gathered again so that they were not even able to eat. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.21: When his family <sup>ⓒ</sup> heard this, they set out to restrain him, because they said, “He’s out of his mind.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.22: The scribes <sup>ⓔ</sup> who had come down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,”  <sup>ⓕ</sup> and, “He drives out <sup>ⓖ</sup> demons <sup>ⓗ</sup> by the ruler <sup>ⓘ</sup> of the demons.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.23: So he summoned <sup>ⓚ</sup> them and spoke to them in parables: <sup>ⓛ</sup> “How can Satan <sup>ⓜ</sup> drive out Satan? <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.24: If a kingdom <sup>ⓐ</sup> is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
CSB17.25: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
CSB17.26: And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand but is finished.
CSB17.27: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his possessions unless he first ties up <sup>ⓑ</sup>the strong man. Then he can plunder his house.
CSB17.28: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for all sins and whatever blasphemies <sup>ⓒ</sup> they utter.
CSB17.29: But whoever blasphemes <sup>ⓓ</sup>against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, <sup>ⓔ</sup>but is guilty <sup>ⓕ</sup>of an eternal sin” <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup>  —
CSB17.30: because they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.31: His <sup>ⓘ</sup> mother <sup>ⓙ</sup> and his brothers <sup>ⓚ</sup> came, and standing outside, they sent word to him and called him.
CSB17.32: A crowd was sitting around him and told him, “Look, your mother, your brothers, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and your sisters <sup>[6]</sup> are outside asking for you.”
CSB17.33: He replied to them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
CSB17.34: Looking at those sitting in a circle around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers! <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.35: Whoever does the will of God <sup>ⓝ</sup>is my brother and sister and mother.”

AMP.1: Again Jesus went into a synagogue; and a man was there whose hand was withered.
AMP.2: The Pharisees were watching Jesus closely to see if He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him [in the Jewish high court].
AMP.3: He said to the man whose hand was withered, "Get up and come forward!"
AMP.4: He asked them, "Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save a life or to kill?" But they kept silent.
AMP.5: After looking around at them with anger, grieved at the hardness and arrogance of their hearts, He told the man, "Hold out your hand." And he held it out, and his hand was [completely] restored.
AMP.6: Then the Pharisees went out and immediately began conspiring with the Herodians [to plot] against Him, as to how they might [fabricate some legal grounds to] put Him to death.
AMP.7: Jesus withdrew to the sea with His disciples; and a large crowd from Galilee followed Him; and also people from Judea,
AMP.8: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumea, and [from the region] beyond the Jordan, and around Tyre and Sidon; a vast number of people came to Him because they were hearing about all [the things] that He was doing.
AMP.9: And He told His disciples to have a small boat stand ready for Him because of the many people, so that they would not crowd Him;
AMP.10: for He had healed many, and as a result all who had diseases pressed around Him to touch Him.
AMP.11: Whenever the unclean spirits saw Him, they fell down before Him and screamed out, "You are the Son of God!"
AMP.12: Jesus sternly warned them [again and again] not to tell who He was.
AMP.13: He went up on the hillside and called those whom He Himself wanted and chose; and they came to Him.
AMP.14: And He appointed twelve [disciples], so that they would be with Him [for instruction] and so that He could send them out to preach [the gospel as apostles—that is, as His special messengers, personally chosen representatives],
AMP.15: and to have authority and power to cast out demons.
AMP.16: He appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom He gave the name Peter),
AMP.17: and James, the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (to them He gave the name Boanerges, that is, "Sons of Thunder");
AMP.18: and [He also appointed] Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew (Nathanael), and Matthew (Levi the tax collector), and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus (Judas the son of James), and Simon the Zealot;
AMP.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Him.
AMP.20: Then He came to a house [in Capernaum], and a crowd formed again, so [many people] that Jesus and His disciples could not even eat a meal [together].
AMP.21: When His own family heard this they went to take custody of Him; for they were saying, "He is out of His mind."
AMP.22: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, "He is possessed by Beelzebul (Satan)," and "He is driving out the demons by the [power of the] ruler of the demons."
AMP.23: So He called them to Himself and spoke to them in parables, "How can Satan drive out Satan?
AMP.24: If a kingdom is divided [split into factions and rebelling] against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
AMP.25: And if a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
AMP.26: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but is coming to an end.
AMP.27: But no one can go into a strong man's house and steal his property unless he first overpowers and ties up the strong man, and then he will ransack and rob his house.
AMP.28: "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, all sins will be forgiven the sons of men, and all the abusive and blasphemous things they say;
AMP.29: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit and His power [by attributing the miracles done by Me to Satan] never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an everlasting sin [a sin which is unforgivable in this present age as well as in the age to come]"—
AMP.30: [Jesus said this] because the scribes and Pharisees were [attributing His miracles to Satan by] saying, "He has an unclean spirit."
AMP.31: Then His mother and His brothers arrived, and standing outside they sent word to Him and called for Him.
AMP.32: A crowd was sitting around Him, and they said to Him, "Look! Your mother and Your brothers are outside asking for You."
AMP.33: And He replied, "Who are My mother and My brothers?"
AMP.34: Looking at those who were sitting in a circle around Him, He said, "Here are My mother and My brothers!
AMP.35: For whoever does the will of God [by believing in Me, and following Me], he is My brother and sister and mother."

MSG.1: Then he went back in the meeting place where he found a man with a crippled hand.
MSG.2: The Pharisees had their eyes on Jesus to see if he would heal him, hoping to catch him in a Sabbath infraction.
MSG.3: He said to the man with the crippled hand, "Stand here where we can see you."
MSG.4: Then he spoke to the people: "What kind of action suits the Sabbath best? Doing good or doing evil? Helping people or leaving them helpless?" No one said a word.
MSG.5: He looked them in the eye, one after another, angry now, furious at their hard-nosed religion. He said to the man, "Hold out your hand." He held it out—it was as good as new!
MSG.6: The Pharisees got out as fast as they could, sputtering about how they would join forces with Herod's followers and ruin him.
MSG.7: Jesus went off with his disciples to the sea to get away. But a huge crowd from Galilee trailed after them—
MSG.8: also from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, across the Jordan, and around Tyre and Sidon—swarms of people who had heard the reports and had come to see for themselves.
MSG.9: He told his disciples to get a boat ready so he wouldn't be trampled by the crowd.
MSG.10: He had healed many people, and now everyone who had something wrong was pushing and shoving to get near and touch him.
MSG.11: Evil spirits, when they recognized him, fell down and cried out, "You are the Son of God!"
MSG.12: But Jesus would have none of it. He shut them up, forbidding them to identify him in public.
MSG.13: He climbed a mountain and invited those he wanted with him. They climbed together.
MSG.14: He settled on twelve, and designated them apostles. The plan was that they would be with him, and he would send them out to proclaim the Word
MSG.15: and give them authority to banish demons.
MSG.16: These are the Twelve: Simon (Jesus later named him Peter, meaning "Rock"),
MSG.17: James, son of Zebedee, John, brother of James (Jesus nicknamed the Zebedee brothers Boanerges, meaning "Sons of Thunder"),
MSG.18: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James, son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Canaanite,
MSG.20: Jesus came home and, as usual, a crowd gathered—so many making demands on him that there wasn't even time to eat.
MSG.21: His friends heard what was going on and went to rescue him, by force if necessary. They suspected he was getting carried away with himself.
MSG.22: The religion scholars from Jerusalem came down spreading rumors that he was working black magic, using devil tricks to impress them with spiritual power.
MSG.23: Jesus confronted their slander with a story: "Does it make sense to send a devil to catch a devil, to use Satan to get rid of Satan?
MSG.24: A constantly squabbling family disintegrates. If Satan were fighting Satan, there soon wouldn't be any Satan left.
MSG.27: Do you think it's possible in broad daylight to enter the house of an awake, able-bodied man, and walk off with his possessions unless you tie him up first? Tie him up, though, and you can clean him out.
MSG.28: "Listen to this carefully. I'm warning you. There's nothing done or said that can't be forgiven.
MSG.29: But if you persist in your slanders against God's Holy Spirit, you are repudiating the very One who forgives, sawing off the branch on which you're sitting, severing by your own perversity all connection with the One who forgives."
MSG.30: He gave this warning because they were accusing him of being in league with Evil.
MSG.31: Just then his mother and brothers showed up. Standing outside, they relayed a message that they wanted a word with him.
MSG.32: He was surrounded by the crowd when he was given the message, "Your mother and brothers and sisters are outside looking for you."
MSG.33: Jesus responded, "Who do you think are my mother and brothers?"
MSG.34: Looking around, taking in everyone seated around him, he said, "Right here, right in front of you—my mother and my brothers.
MSG.35: Obedience is thicker than blood. The person who obeys God's will is my brother and sister and mother."

NIV.1: Another time he went into the synagogue, and a man with a shriveled hand was there.
NIV.2: Some of them were looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, so they watched him closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath.
NIV.3: Jesus said to the man with the shriveled hand, “Stand up in front of everyone.”
NIV.4: Then Jesus asked them, “Which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?” But they remained silent.
NIV.5: He looked around at them in anger and, deeply distressed at their stubborn hearts, said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was completely restored.
NIV.6: Then the Pharisees went out and began to plot with the Herodians how they might kill Jesus.
NIV.7:
Crowds Follow Jesus
Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the lake, and a large crowd from Galilee followed.
NIV.8: When they heard all he was doing, many people came to him from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, and the regions across the Jordan and around Tyre and Sidon.
NIV.9: Because of the crowd he told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him, to keep the people from crowding him.
NIV.10: For he had healed many, so that those with diseases were pushing forward to touch him.
NIV.11: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God.”
NIV.12: But he gave them strict orders not to tell who he was.
NIV.13:
The Appointing of the Twelve Apostles
Jesus went up on a mountainside and called to him those he wanted, and they came to him.
NIV.14: He appointed twelve—designating them apostles—that they might be with him and that he might send them out to preach
NIV.15: and to have authority to drive out demons.
NIV.16: These are the twelve he appointed: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
NIV.17: James son of Zebedee and his brother John (to them he gave the name Boanerges, which means Sons of Thunder);
NIV.18: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot
NIV.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
NIV.20:
Jesus and Beelzebub
Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat.
NIV.21: When his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.”
NIV.22: And the teachers of the law who came down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebub! By the prince of demons he is driving out demons.”
NIV.23: So Jesus called them and spoke to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan?
NIV.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
NIV.25: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
NIV.26: And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand; his end has come.
NIV.27: In fact, no one can enter a strong man’s house and carry off his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can rob his house.
NIV.28: I tell you the truth, all the sins and blasphemies of men will be forgiven them.
NIV.29: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; he is guilty of an eternal sin.”
NIV.30: He said this because they were saying, “He has an evil spirit.”
NIV.31:
Jesus’ Mother and Brothers
Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him.
NIV.32: A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.”
NIV.33: “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked.
NIV.34: Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
NIV.35: Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”

NRSVCE.1: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
NRSVCE.2: They watched him to see whether he would cure him on the sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
NRSVCE.3: And he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come forward.”
NRSVCE.4: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
NRSVCE.5: He looked around at them with anger; he was grieved at their hardness of heart and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
NRSVCE.6: The Pharisees went out and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
NRSVCE.7: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him;
NRSVCE.8: hearing all that he was doing, they came to him in great numbers from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the region around Tyre and Sidon.
NRSVCE.9: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him;
NRSVCE.10: for he had cured many, so that all who had diseases pressed upon him to touch him.
NRSVCE.11: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and shouted, “You are the Son of God!”
NRSVCE.12: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.
NRSVCE.13: He went up the mountain and called to him those whom he wanted, and they came to him.
NRSVCE.14: And he appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, and to be sent out to proclaim the message,
NRSVCE.15: and to have authority to cast out demons.
NRSVCE.16: So he appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
NRSVCE.17: James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
NRSVCE.18: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,
NRSVCE.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him. Jesus and Beelzebul Then he went home;
NRSVCE.20: and the crowd came together again, so that they could not even eat.
NRSVCE.21: When his family heard it, they went out to restrain him, for people were saying, “He has gone out of his mind.”
NRSVCE.22: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of the demons he casts out demons.”
NRSVCE.23: And he called them to him, and spoke to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
NRSVCE.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
NRSVCE.25: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
NRSVCE.26: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but his end has come.
NRSVCE.27: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property without first tying up the strong man; then indeed the house can be plundered.
NRSVCE.28: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for their sins and whatever blasphemies they utter;
NRSVCE.29: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit can never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin” —
NRSVCE.30: for they had said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
NRSVCE.31: Then his mother and his brothers came; and standing outside, they sent to him and called him.
NRSVCE.32: A crowd was sitting around him; and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, asking for you.”
NRSVCE.33: And he replied, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
NRSVCE.34: And looking at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
NRSVCE.35: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”

NET.1: Then Jesus entered the synagogue again, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
NET.2: They watched Jesus closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they could accuse him.
NET.3: So he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Stand up among all these people.”
NET.4: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good on the Sabbath, or evil, to save a life or destroy it?” But they were silent.
NET.5: After looking around at them in anger, grieved by the hardness of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
NET.6: So the Pharisees went out immediately and began plotting with the Herodians, as to how they could assassinate him.
NET.7: Then Jesus went away with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him. And from Judea,
NET.8: Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan River, and around Tyre and Sidon a great multitude came to him when they heard about the things he had done.
NET.9: Because of the crowd, he told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him so the crowd would not press toward him.
NET.10: For he had healed many, so that all who were afflicted with diseases pressed toward him in order to touch him.
NET.11: And whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God.”
NET.12: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.
NET.13: Now Jesus went up the mountain and called for those he wanted, and they came to him.
NET.14: He appointed twelve (whom he named apostles ), so that they would be with him and he could send them to preach
NET.15: and to have authority to cast out demons.
NET.16: He appointed twelve: To Simon he gave the name Peter;
NET.17: to James and his brother John, the sons of Zebedee, he gave the name Boanerges (that is, “sons of thunder”);
NET.18: and Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot,
NET.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
NET.20: Now Jesus went home, and a crowd gathered so that they were not able to eat.
NET.21: When his family heard this they went out to restrain him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.”
NET.22: The experts in the law who came down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and, “By the ruler of demons he casts out demons.”
NET.23: So he called them and spoke to them in parables: “How can Satan cast out Satan?
NET.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom will not be able to stand.
NET.25: If a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
NET.26: And if Satan rises against himself and is divided, he is not able to stand and his end has come.
NET.27: But no one is able to enter a strong man’s house and steal his property unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can thoroughly plunder his house.
NET.28: I tell you the truth, people will be forgiven for all sins, even all the blasphemies they utter.
NET.29: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven, but is guilty of an eternal sin”
NET.30: (because they said, “He has an unclean spirit” ).
NET.31: Then Jesus’ mother and his brothers came. Standing outside, they sent word to him, to summon him.
NET.32: A crowd was sitting around him and they said to him, “Look, your mother and your brothers are outside looking for you.”
NET.33: He answered them and said, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
NET.34: And looking at those who were sitting around him in a circle, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
NET.35: For whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
